ocr.text: The table presents the frequency of participant choices to buy either net A or net B, detailing the number of choices made out of
a maximum of 7. For the row labeled 0 choices, the percent is 7.8% with a corresponding frequency of 62. In the row for 1
choice, the percent is 8.0% and the frequency is 64. For participants making 2 choices, the percent stands at 7.0% with a
frequency of 56. In the case of 3 choices, the percent is 8.3% and the frequency is 66. The row for 4 choices indicates a percent
Number of times participant chose to buy either net A or net B (out of 7 maximum)  Frequency
When participants made 5 choices, the percent recorded is 8.9% and the frequency is 71. For 6 choices, the percent is 11.0%
and the frequency is 88. Notably, in the row for 7 choices, the percent is significantly higher at 39.7% with a frequency of 316.
Finally, the aggregate row labeled "All" summarizes the total percent as 100.0% with a cumulative frequency of 796. This data
illustrates the distribution of choices among participants, revealing a notable increase in frequency as the number of choices
rises, particularly peaking at 7 choices. The structured breakdown of each category effectively conveys the trends in participant
decision-making behavior regarding net A and net B purchases across the defined parameters.
